title: Content Guide shown for submitters in Forms
excerpt:

posts.author: Jodifactors123 (Jodi)
posts.cookedHtml:
Description

Would like users to see the table of contents on forms with pages. It would make for quick navigation to information that is relevant for that submission. We have long forms with multiple pages, but not all pages are relevant per submission.

What are you trying to achieve

A way for users to navigate through a lengthy form to pick only the relevant sections needed for a particular submission

posts.author: ProjectMastermind (IngenuityKat)
posts.cookedHtml:
I get what you’re going for here, but this is usually better solved with conditional logic.

Instead of helping users navigate a long form, the goal should be to only show them what’s relevant in the first place. Using conditions to reveal sections based on earlier answers keeps the experience much cleaner and avoids unnecessary pages altogether.

That said, there is still a gap with page level control and navigation, so longer forms can feel clunky even when conditions are used.

If you have access to Vibe, you could also build exactly what you’re describing with a more dynamic, guided experience.
